sourceMovie.title: A Haunted House 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Haunted House 2 (2014) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Michael Tiddes's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fright Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fright Night (1985) operates in the same comedy as A Haunted House 2, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Fido
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fido (2006) belongs to the same conversation as A Haunted House 2 — both anchor their comedy in horror emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Night of the Demons
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Haunted House 2's comedy stayed with you, Night of the Demons (1988) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The horror through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Extra Ordinary
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where A Haunted House 2 excels in comedy, Extra Ordinary (2019) doubles down on horror to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Conjuring 2 (2016) is the natural next step after A Haunted House 2: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and craft elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Exorcist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of A Haunted House 2 who respond to its horror will find The Exorcist (1973) immediately familiar. Both films share a dark sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason A Haunted House 2 fans keep landing on Beetlejuice (1988) — the comedy is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Insidious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Insidious draws from the same horror well as A Haunted House 2, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2011 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: This Is the End
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: This Is the End (2013) captures the comedy appeal of A Haunted House 2 without simply imitating it. The action streak running through both films is what puts This Is the End at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Annabelle: Creation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror appeal of A Haunted House 2 — that dark edge, the craft — translates cleanly into Annabelle: Creation (2017). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Haunted House 2?
faq.answer: Try Fright Night, Fido, Night of the Demons—each shares DNA with A Haunted House 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Haunted House 2?
faq.answer: A Haunted House 2 (2014) is best described as Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Haunted House 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "A Haunted House Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
